MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Parsons Government Services, Inc. FKA Parsons Infrastructure & Technology Group, Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to show that Plaintiff does not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that he was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to provide the Court with a complete set of Plaintiff's responses to Defendant's special interrogatories. (Hopwood Decl., Ex E.) Further, co-worker Don Gosney testified that Parsons and Fluor crewmembers destroyed asbestos insulation off pipes to get from one side of the pipeway to the other. (Rose Decl., Ex. A at 56:1-60:14.) Gosney testified that he worked within 50 feet of Plaintiff, other trades employed by Parsons may have worked within 20 feet of Plaintiff, and Plaintiff had to walk through areas where Parsons employees worked to get to the parking lot. (Rose Decl., Ex. A at 107:21-109:20; 114:25-115:16.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended: Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is June 28, 2021 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is July 9, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 24, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is June 11, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 25, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than April 12, 2021. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
